claim_text: In this bounded CPU suite, CCR-MPC reduced mean violation rate from 1.1% for vanilla MPPI to 0.4%, tying the lowest aggregate violation rate but not dominating CVaR/RA-MPPI in cost.
tested_scope: Six simplified low-dimensional CPU domains, three OOD levels, configured seeds.
comparison_class: Vanilla MPPI, CVaR/RA-MPPI, conformal prediction MPC, robust MPC, and uncertainty/risk baselines under matched candidate budgets.
compute_budget: Single-machine CPU run; see configs/execution_config.json and compute_time_by_method.png.
limitations: Not a high-fidelity robot or hardware result.
strongest_alternative_explanation: CCR-MPC does not dominate CVaR/RA-MPPI or conformal-prediction MPC on all metrics.

claim_text: CCR combines counterfactual regret, violation tails, margin tails, rank instability, and action disagreement before split calibration.
tested_scope: Sampled MPC candidate sets with learned dynamics particles.
comparison_class: Non-CCR conformal risk, prediction-calibrated uncertainty, disagreement-only, regret-only, and violation-tail-only ablations.
compute_budget: Candidate budget and horizon in configs/execution_config.json.
limitations: Calibration assumes the simplified calibration contexts represent evaluation contexts.
strongest_alternative_explanation: A simpler calibrated violation-rate score may explain some gains.

claim_text: The combined CCR score matched the low violation rate of regret-only and violation-tail-only gates while using substantially lower cost and freezing in the aggregate CPU suite.
tested_scope: Aggregate over the bounded CPU suite.
comparison_class: CCR no calibration, calibration no CCR, disagreement-only, regret-only, violation-tail-only.
compute_budget: Same action-candidate budget and dynamics-particle count.
limitations: Ablation ordering may change with tuning or richer domains.
strongest_alternative_explanation: Tuning differences, not the feature combination, may account for some differences.

claim_text: CCR-MPC should be positioned as a decision-risk statistic and calibrated selection rule, not as a method that empirically dominates all risk-aware or conformal MPC baselines.
tested_scope: Focused CPU suite and verified related-work metadata.
comparison_class: CVaR/RA-MPPI, robust MPC, conformal risk/decision/prediction baselines.
compute_budget: Same focused CPU run; citation sources recorded in reports/citation_verification.md.
limitations: The distinction is partly conceptual because the strongest baselines are competitive in this suite.
strongest_alternative_explanation: Reviewer may reasonably view CCR as a calibrated score variant unless richer learned-dynamics settings strengthen the empirical gap.

claim_text: Prediction calibration alone does not imply control-risk calibration; a decision boundary can amplify small model differences into unsafe MPC actions.
tested_scope: Constructive argument plus simplified synthetic domain.
compute_budget: No special hardware; stylized theorem plus CPU synthetic experiment.
limitations: The theorem is a narrow one-step construction, not a general robotics safety theorem.
strongest_alternative_explanation: The synthetic construction may be too stylized to explain all observed domains.
